problem: What is the remainder when $2^{2005}$ is divided by 7?

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why we are starting from $2^1$ and considering the remainder when successive powers of 2 are divided by 7?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What pattern do you notice in the remainders when we divide successive powers of 2 by 7?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How can we use this pattern to determine the remainder when $2^{2005}$ is divided by 7?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the significance of the fact that the pattern repeats every 3 powers of 2?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why we can use the remainder of $2^3$ (which is 1) to find the remainder of $2^{2005}$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Based on the pattern we observed, what is the remainder when $2^{2005}$ is divided by 7?
classified_questions.label: b
